title: React.js Simple 30-Day Roadmap 🚀
subtitle: A beginner-friendly, structured guide to mastering React.js in 4 weeks with daily 2-hour consistent milestones. Covers practical core concepts without unnecessary complexity.

sections.label: Daily Tasks

days.taskTitle: React Ki ar Keno Sikhbo?
days.taskSub: Normal HTML/JS er sathe React er parthokko bujha. Vite diye ekta brand new React project setup kora ar npm run dev diye run kora step by step sikhbo.

days.taskTitle: JSX ki ar etar Rules
days.taskSub: JavaScript er vitor HTML er moto kore code lekha-i holo JSX. Rules gula sahaj: sobo kichu ekta parent tag er vitor thakte hobe ar class er bodle className likhte hobe.

days.taskTitle: Components Banano
days.taskSub: Component mane UI er choto choto tukro ( jemon Navbar, Sidebar, Card). Ekta alada file-e function banaye sheta kivebe main App design-e use kortey hoy sheta dekhbo.

days.taskTitle: Props diye Data Pass kora
days.taskSub: Ekta Component theke arekta Component-e data pathanor upay holo Props. Java ba C++ er function argument er moto cinta korte paro. Variable ba string props hisebe pathaye receive kora sikhbo.

days.taskTitle: Map diye List Render kora
days.taskSub: Ekta array te onk gula data thakle sheta .map() loop chalaye UI te list akare dekhano sikhbo. Proti ta item er jonno ekta unique key prop dite hoy, sheta mathay rakhbo.

days.taskTitle: Conditional Rendering (UI change kora)
days.taskSub: Kono shorto ba condition er upor vitti kore UI bodlano. Ternary operator condition ? true : false ba && and operator use kore login/logout button toggle kora dekhbo.

days.taskTitle: Week 1 Choto Project
days.taskSub: Eto din ja sikhlam ta diye ekta static User Profile Card grid banano jekhane map ar props use hobe.
days.projectBox: Choto Target: Ekta products page banano jekhane static array theke product card dekhaba conditions shoho.

sections.label: Daily Tasks

days.taskTitle: Click Event Handle Kora
days.taskSub: React-e button-e click korle kibhabe ekta function trigger korte hoy dekhbo. onClick={myFunction} syntax ta run korbo bracket chara.

days.taskTitle: useState Hook er Mul dharona
days.taskSub: Normal variable change korle React-er UI bodlay na. UI-er data active vabe change korar jonnoy useState lage. State holo React er nijer memory storage.

days.taskTitle: Counter ar Toggle Banano
days.taskSub: Ekta button click korle sonkha barbe ar kombe (Counter App) ebong arekta button click korle text dekhabe ba lukiye felbe (Toggle functionality) eita prothome practice korbo.

days.taskTitle: Input Field Handling
days.taskSub: Form field ba input box e kichu likhle sheta state-e kibhabe capture korte hoy onChange event ar e.target.value use kore sheta sikhbo.

days.taskTitle: Array & Object State Update kora
days.taskSub: State e jodi array ba object thake sheta directly change kora jay na. Spread operator ... diye ager data copy kore notun data jog korar niyom sikhbo.

days.taskTitle: Lifting State Up
days.taskSub: Jodi duita alada sibling component-er moddhe data share korte hoy, tokhon state take tader common parent element-e niye jete hoy. Sahaj vabe eta practice korbo.

days.taskTitle: Week 2 Mini Project: Todo App
days.taskSub: State ar Input combined use kore ekta simple Todo list jekhane item add ar delete kora jabe.
days.projectBox: Choto Target: Ekta safe state dynamic Todo app build kore GitHub-e safe commit kora.

sections.label: Daily Tasks

days.taskTitle: useEffect Hook Ki ar Keno Lage?
days.taskSub: Component jokhon monitor-e prothom bar load hoy (Mount) tokhon jodi kono kaaj auto chalate cai (jemon data load kora), tar jonno useEffect use hoy.

days.taskTitle: Fetch API diye Data Load kora
days.taskSub: useEffect er vitor dynamic fetch() block likhe JSON Placeholder data load kore sheta console log ba state-e rakha dekhbo.

days.taskTitle: Loading status ar Error state
days.taskSub: API call-e loading state toggle kora jate load hobar somoy screen-e "Loading..." lekha othe ar net fail korle error warning message dekhano jay.

days.taskTitle: Dependency Array closely bujha
days.taskSub: useEffect er sheshe je bracket [] thake sheta bujha. Empty thakle ekbar colbe, vitor kono variable thakle sheta change holei shudu reload hobe. Infinite loop avoid kora sikhbo.

days.taskTitle: LocalStorage backup integration
days.taskSub: State data change hole sheta sathe sathe browser er temporary memory checking slot localStorage e save kore rakha jate refresh dileo data na haray.

days.taskTitle: Week 3 Project: User Directory Dashboard
days.taskSub: Ekta endpoint theke user list load kore cards layout-e visual load screen shoho map kora.
days.projectBox: Choto Target: Live users summary dashboard load grid loading flags shoho banano.

title: 3-Month ML Roadmap
subtitle: Daily 1.5 hrs · Corey Schafer + supplementary resources · C++/Java background assumed

tabs.name: Month 1 — Foundations

phases.title: Week 1 — Python Syntax Through ML Lens

sections.label: Corey Schafer playlist — videos to watch

days.taskTitle: Setup + Variables + Data Types
days.taskSub: Why Python? How it differs from Java/C++. Install Anaconda + VS Code.

days.taskTitle: Strings + Lists + Indexing
days.taskSub: ML connection: datasets are lists of lists. Practice slicing.

days.taskTitle: Dictionaries + Sets + Tuples
days.taskSub: Hyperparameters live in dicts. Build a model config dict.

days.taskTitle: Conditionals + Loops
days.taskSub: Training = a loop. Simulate 10 epochs printing fake loss values.

days.taskTitle: Functions + *args, **kwargs
days.taskSub: Every ML preprocessing step is a function. Write normalize(), split().

days.taskTitle: Classes + OOP (you know this!)
days.taskSub: sklearn models are classes. Build your own DataSplitter class.

days.taskTitle: Mini Project: Manual Regression Calculator
days.taskSub: Pure Python, no libraries. Calculate slope, intercept, predict, compute MSE.

sections.note: 📺 Corey Schafer videos #1–10, #15–16. You already know OOP, so #15–16 will be fast. Skip #11–14 (file I/O, covered later).

phases.title: Week 2 — NumPy: The Engine of ML Math

sections.label: Resource: Keith Galli — Complete NumPy Tutorial (YouTube, 1 video ~2hrs)

days.taskTitle: Arrays, shape, dtype, creating arrays
days.taskSub: np.array, np.zeros, np.ones, np.random.randn, .shape

days.taskTitle: Indexing, slicing, boolean indexing
days.taskSub: Filter samples where label == 1. Core ML data manipulation.

days.taskTitle: Math operations + vectorization
days.taskSub: Element-wise ops, dot product, matrix multiply. Why loops are slow.

days.taskTitle: Reshape, transpose, broadcasting
days.taskSub: Critical for deep learning. Reshape (100,) → (100,1). Understand broadcasting.

days.taskTitle: Statistical functions + random seed
days.taskSub: np.mean, std, corrcoef. Always set np.random.seed(42)!

days.taskTitle: Matrix operations (linalg)
days.taskSub: np.dot, np.matmul (@), np.linalg.inv, .T — used in every neural net.

days.taskTitle: Mini Project: Linear Regression with Normal Equation
days.taskSub: θ = (XᵀX)⁻¹ Xᵀy — implement from scratch using only NumPy.

sections.label: Corey Schafer Pandas Series (separate playlist) — 11 videos

days.taskTitle: DataFrame basics — load, head, info, describe
days.taskSub: Load Titanic CSV. Understand rows=samples, cols=features.

days.taskTitle: Selecting + filtering data
days.taskSub: df[col], df[df[col] > x], .loc, .iloc — the 4 selection methods.

days.taskTitle: Missing values + data cleaning
days.taskSub: isnull(), dropna(), fillna(). Handle Titanic Age, Cabin columns.

days.taskTitle: Feature engineering + apply()
days.taskSub: Create FamilySize, IsAlone, AgeGroup. The biggest ML skill.

days.taskTitle: Encoding categoricals + groupby
days.taskSub: get_dummies(), groupby().mean(). Survival rate by class.

days.taskTitle: Merge + concat + to NumPy
days.taskSub: Combine datasets. Export X, y arrays for sklearn.

days.taskTitle: Mini Project: Titanic Full EDA
days.taskSub: Load → Clean → Engineer 3 features → Group analysis → Export clean CSV.

sections.label: Corey Schafer Matplotlib series + StatQuest (Josh Starmer)

days.taskTitle: Matplotlib basics — line, scatter, hist
days.taskSub: Plot training loss over epochs. Always add labels + title.

days.taskTitle: Subplots + figure customization
days.taskSub: fig, ax pattern. Multiple plots in one figure.

days.taskTitle: Seaborn — heatmap, pairplot, boxplot
days.taskSub: Correlation heatmap on Titanic. Detect outliers with boxplot.

days.taskTitle: Statistics — mean, std, IQR, distributions
days.taskSub: StatQuest: "Statistics Fundamentals" playlist. First 3 videos.

days.taskTitle: Correlation + Bayes theorem
days.taskSub: Pearson r. Bayes intuition for spam filters. StatQuest Bayes video.

days.taskTitle: Probability distributions + t-test
days.taskSub: Normal dist, p-value. When is model A actually better than B?

days.taskTitle: Linear Algebra basics — vectors, matrices
days.taskSub: 3Blue1Brown: "Essence of Linear Algebra" — watch videos 1–4.

days.taskTitle: Matrix multiply + PCA intuition
days.taskSub: 3Blue1Brown videos 5–9. How PCA works geometrically.

days.taskTitle: Month 1 Project: Complete Visual EDA Report
days.taskSub: Any Kaggle dataset → full EDA notebook with 8+ charts + written findings.

sections.note: 🎯 Month 1 End Goal: Comfortable with Python, NumPy, Pandas. Can load, clean, visualize any dataset. Have 3 mini projects + 1 EDA report on GitHub.

tabs.milestone: Month 1 milestone: First GitHub repo + EDA notebook live

tabs.name: Month 2 — Core ML

sections.label: Resource: Kaggle Learn — Feature Engineering course (free, interactive)

days.taskTitle: Scalers — StandardScaler, MinMaxScaler
days.taskSub: CRITICAL: fit on train only, transform both. Data leakage explained.

days.taskTitle: Advanced imputation — KNNImputer, indicator features
days.taskSub: Missing value itself can be a feature! Add is_missing column.

days.taskTitle: Encoding — OHE, Label, Ordinal
days.taskSub: When to use each. Handle_unknown='ignore' for production.

days.taskTitle: Feature creation — log transform, binning, interaction
days.taskSub: np.log1p() for skewed data. pd.cut() for age groups.

days.taskTitle: Outlier detection + removal (IQR, Z-score)
days.taskSub: Build a reusable detect_outliers() function.

days.taskTitle: sklearn Pipeline + ColumnTransformer
days.taskSub: The professional way to preprocess. One pipeline, no leakage.

days.taskTitle: Mini Project: Auto DataCleaner class
days.taskSub: Takes raw df → returns X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test. Clean and scaled.

sections.label: Resource: StatQuest ML Fundamentals playlist + sklearn docs

days.taskTitle: Linear Regression — theory + code
days.taskSub: StatQuest: "Linear Regression" video. Train on California Housing. RMSE, R².

days.taskTitle: Ridge + Lasso — regularization
days.taskSub: Why overfit? L1 vs L2. Plot coefficients as alpha changes.

days.taskTitle: Logistic Regression — binary classification
days.taskSub: Sigmoid, decision threshold. Train on Titanic. Confusion matrix.

days.taskTitle: Decision Tree — theory + visualization
days.taskSub: plot_tree(). Max_depth experiment: see overfitting in real time.

days.taskTitle: Random Forest — ensemble power
days.taskSub: Feature importance bar chart. n_jobs=-1 for speed. Bagging concept.

days.taskTitle: SVM + KNN — distance-based models
days.taskSub: ALWAYS scale first! Kernel trick intuition. KNN elbow for k.

days.taskTitle: XGBoost — the Kaggle king
days.taskSub: pip install xgboost. Early stopping. Compare vs Random Forest.

sections.label: StatQuest + Kaggle Getting Started competitions

days.taskTitle: Metrics — accuracy vs F1 vs ROC-AUC
days.taskSub: Imbalanced data kills accuracy. When to use which metric.

days.taskTitle: Confusion matrix + classification report
days.taskSub: TP, FP, FN, TN. What each quadrant means for your model.

days.taskTitle: Cross-validation — StratifiedKFold
days.taskSub: Why a single train/test split lies. CV score = reliable truth.

days.taskTitle: GridSearchCV + RandomizedSearchCV
days.taskSub: Systematic hyperparameter tuning. Best params on Random Forest.

days.taskTitle: Learning curves — diagnose bias vs variance
days.taskSub: Plot train vs val accuracy. Identify overfit vs underfit visually.

days.taskTitle: Month 2 Project: Algorithm Comparison Framework
days.taskSub: 6 algorithms, same dataset, comparison table + charts. Best auto-picked.

days.taskTitle: Kaggle: Titanic competition — submit!
days.taskSub: Join kaggle.com → Titanic competition → full pipeline → first submission.

days.taskTitle: Kaggle: House Prices — submit!
days.taskSub: Regression. Feature engineering focus. Aim for top 40%.

tabs.milestone: Month 2 milestone: 2 Kaggle submissions + Algorithm Comparison project on GitHub

tabs.name: Month 3 — Deep Learning & Deploy

sections.label: Resource: 3Blue1Brown Neural Networks series (4 videos) + Michael Nielsen book

days.taskTitle: What is a neuron? Forward pass math
days.taskSub: 3B1B video 1. Manually compute output = W·x + b → sigmoid. No code yet.

days.taskTitle: Gradient descent — the learning algorithm
days.taskSub: 3B1B video 2. Loss surface, slope, stepping downhill. Intuition first.

days.taskTitle: Backpropagation — chain rule
days.taskSub: 3B1B video 3+4. This is the heart of all of deep learning.

days.taskTitle: Code: Forward pass in NumPy
days.taskSub: Implement sigmoid, relu, forward() from scratch. Layer by layer.

days.taskTitle: Code: Backpropagation in NumPy
days.taskSub: Implement backward(). Manual gradient update. Watch loss drop.

days.taskTitle: Activations, loss functions, learning rate
days.taskSub: ReLU vs sigmoid. BCE vs MSE. LR too high/low — experiment.

days.taskTitle: Mini Project: MNIST classifier in pure NumPy
days.taskSub: 784→128→64→10. No PyTorch. Achieve >92% accuracy. Plot loss curve.

sections.label: Resource: Patrick Loeber — PyTorch Tutorial for Beginners (YouTube, 20 videos)

days.taskTitle: Tensors + autograd
days.taskSub: Tensor = NumPy array + GPU + gradient tracking. requires_grad=True.

days.taskTitle: nn.Module — build your first network
days.taskSub: nn.Sequential, nn.Linear, nn.ReLU, nn.Dropout, nn.BatchNorm1d.

days.taskTitle: Loss functions + optimizers
days.taskSub: BCEWithLogitsLoss, CrossEntropyLoss. Adam optimizer. LR schedulers.

days.taskTitle: The training loop — full pattern
days.taskSub: zero_grad → forward → loss → backward → step. This is the core loop forever.

days.taskTitle: DataLoader + custom Dataset class
days.taskSub: Batch training. num_workers, shuffle. Build CustomDataset from scratch.

days.taskTitle: Early stopping + model save/load
days.taskSub: Save best model. torch.save(state_dict). Load for inference.

days.taskTitle: Mini Project: MNIST in PyTorch + Fraud Detection
days.taskSub: MLP 784→512→256→10 (>98% acc). Then tabular fraud dataset ROC-AUC >0.90.

sections.label: Resource: fast.ai Practical Deep Learning (free) + HuggingFace NLP course (free)

days.taskTitle: CNN architecture — Conv2d, MaxPool, BatchNorm
days.taskSub: What a convolution does visually. Build SimpleCNN from scratch.

days.taskTitle: Transfer learning — ResNet18 fine-tuning
days.taskSub: 3 lines to a 90%+ classifier. Freeze layers, replace head, train.

days.taskTitle: Mini CV Project: Image classifier (your own 3 classes)
days.taskSub: Collect 50 photos each of 3 things. Fine-tune ResNet18. Build Gradio demo.

days.taskTitle: NLP — text preprocessing + TF-IDF
days.taskSub: Tokenize, remove stopwords, lemmatize. TF-IDF vectorizer for spam.

days.taskTitle: Transformers + HuggingFace pipeline
days.taskSub: 3 lines for sentiment analysis! Fine-tune DistilBERT on IMDB.

days.taskTitle: Mini NLP Project: Sentiment analyzer
days.taskSub: TF-IDF + LR baseline vs BERT. Compare accuracy + speed. Streamlit demo.

sections.label: Resource: Made With ML (madewithml.com) + Corey Schafer Git series

days.taskTitle: Git + GitHub for ML engineers
days.taskSub: CS Git series #1–4. init, add, commit, push, branch. .gitignore for ML.

days.taskTitle: sklearn Pipeline + joblib save/load
days.taskSub: Build full preprocessing + model pipeline. Save to .pkl. Load in new script.

days.taskTitle: FastAPI — REST endpoint for your model
days.taskSub: @app.post('/predict'). Pydantic input/output. Test with curl.

days.taskTitle: Streamlit — web app in 20 minutes
days.taskSub: st.slider, st.button, st.metric. Deploy your best model as a web app.

days.taskTitle: MLflow — experiment tracking
days.taskSub: Log params, metrics, artifacts. Compare 3 model runs. Load best model.

days.taskTitle: Docker basics — containerize your API
days.taskSub: Dockerfile for FastAPI app. Build + run. This is industry standard.

days.taskTitle: Deploy to HuggingFace Spaces — live URL!
days.taskSub: Push Streamlit app. Get a public link. Share with the world.

days.taskTitle: Final Capstone: End-to-End ML System
days.taskSub: Real Kaggle dataset → Full pipeline → MLflow tracking → FastAPI → Streamlit → Deployed.

days.taskTitle: Portfolio cleanup + LinkedIn update + GitHub polish
days.taskSub: README for every project. Pin best 3 repos. Update LinkedIn with live demo links.

sections.note: 🎯 Day 90 Goal: 5+ GitHub projects, 2 Kaggle submissions, 1 live deployed app, LinkedIn updated. You are now a Junior ML Engineer candidate.

tabs.milestone: Month 3 milestone: Live deployed ML app + full portfolio + job-ready

days.taskTitle: Sentry Telemetry Runtime Exception Interception Injections
days.taskSub: Track live apps for production bugs, pushing instant event alert feeds to your developer dashboard.

days.taskTitle: Ultimate Capstone Design Framework (Full Stack Integration System)
days.taskSub: Map out comprehensive structural data paths linking mobile apps, databases, and api nodes.

days.taskTitle: Authentication Endpoints Handshakes & Shared Model Wiring Checks
days.taskSub: Set up secure dual token verification routes connecting client layouts to authorization servers.

days.taskTitle: Bidirectional Socket Linkages & Data Flow Synchronization Actions
days.taskSub: Verify socket transmission channels to ensure client elements update smoothly on live server events.

days.taskTitle: Third Party Sandbox Mashups & Secure Terminal Transactions Logs
days.taskSub: Link checkout controllers fields to sandbox payment routes to verify transactional tasks.

days.taskTitle: Comprehensive Readme Design, Visual System Layouts Mapping
days.taskSub: Draft clean architecture diagrams detailing data pipelines paths, pinning links to your hosted assets.

days.taskTitle: Algorithmic Problem Matrices Prep & System Scalability Reviews
days.taskSub: Practice array and string search challenges, and study scaling strategies for large systems.

days.taskTitle: ATS-Optimized Technical Resume Compilation & Digital Launch
days.taskSub: Highlight your system metrics and technology stack, update your professional portfolio, and lock down live repos.

tabs.milestone: 🏁 Full Curriculum Pipeline Terminated: All application stacks deployed. Full engineering portfolio market-ready.

title: UI/UX Design Beginner → Industry-Ready
subtitle: 14 modules · 16 weeks · Figma mastery · Portfolio-ready · Built for Moheuddin

tabs.name: Phase 1 — Beginner

phases.title: Module 1 — Design Thinking & The Designer's Mindset

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 1 to 5

days.taskTitle: UI vs UX — What's the difference?
days.taskSub: UX = the architecture (how it works). UI = the decoration (how it looks). Neither works without the other.

days.taskTitle: Design Thinking — 5 Stages (Empathize → Define → Ideate → Prototype → Test)
days.taskSub: Learn the most important mental model in all of UX. Understand why this is a loop, not a line.

days.taskTitle: The Double Diamond Model + Team Roles
days.taskSub: Discover → Define → Develop → Deliver. Learn who does what: UX Researcher, UI Designer, Product Designer, IxD.

days.taskTitle: The Designer's Toolkit — Overview
days.taskSub: Figma, Whimsical, FigJam, Maze, Hotjar. Install Figma today (free at figma.com).

days.taskTitle: Watch: AJ&Smart — UI vs UX + CareerFoundry — UX Explained
days.taskSub: Both under 15 min total. Take notes: write 3 things you learned in your own words.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 6 to 7

days.taskTitle: Exercise 1A — Empathy Audit (30 min)
days.taskSub: Pick any app you use daily. Write 5 frustrations + 5 delights. Turn each frustration into "As a user, I struggle to ___ because ___."

days.taskTitle: Exercise 1B — Design Scavenger Hunt (45 min)
days.taskSub: Analyze Gmail, Uber, TikTok: Where is the primary action button? How does onboarding work? What happens on error?

days.taskTitle: Project Zero — "Redesign a Bad Experience"
days.taskSub: Find a real frustrating experience. Write a 1-page brief: Who is the user? What is their goal? What is the problem? What does success look like? This becomes your first portfolio case study seed.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 1 (10 questions)
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 1" for 10 questions with instant feedback.

sections.note: 💡 Claude Command: "Review my work: [describe your brief]" — paste your Project Zero brief and get professional feedback before moving on.

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 8 to 12

days.taskTitle: The 7 Gestalt Principles — Part 1 (Proximity, Similarity, Continuity, Closure)
days.taskSub: These are laws of how the human brain sees. Every layout decision you make activates them whether you want it to or not.

days.taskTitle: Gestalt Principles — Part 2 (Figure/Ground, Common Fate, Prägnanz) + Visual Hierarchy
days.taskSub: Guide the user's eye. Tools: Size, Weight, Color, Contrast, Spacing, Position. Rule: one clear primary element per screen.

days.taskTitle: The CRAP Principles — Contrast, Repetition, Alignment, Proximity
days.taskSub: The most actionable design framework for beginners. Learn each with a real UI example.

days.taskTitle: Layout & Grids — 12-column grid, 8pt system, Rule of Thirds, Whitespace
days.taskSub: All spacing in multiples of 8: 8, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64. Whitespace is a design element, not wasted space.

days.taskTitle: Basic UI Components — Cards, Modals, Tabs, Toasts, Accordions
days.taskSub: Learn what each component is for. Using them correctly matters more than making them pretty.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 13 to 14

days.taskTitle: Exercise 2A — Visual Hierarchy Sketch (30 min)
days.taskSub: On paper: design a news article card (headline, author, date, image, description, "Read More"). Make clear what the user reads first, second, third. No design tools yet.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 2B — CRAP Audit (45 min)
days.taskSub: Open any news website. Find 1 good example and 1 violation of each CRAP principle. Document all 8 findings.

days.taskTitle: Project 1-A — "News App Card UI" (3 Variations)
days.taskSub: Design 3 cards: A) Poor hierarchy, B) Good hierarchy bad spacing, C) Polished. Write 3 sentences explaining what improved each time.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 2 (10 questions)
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 2".

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 15 to 19

days.taskTitle: Typography Fundamentals — Typeface vs Font, Classifications, Type Scale
days.taskSub: Use a modular scale: 12, 14, 16, 20, 24, 32, 48px. Never pick random sizes. Line height for body: 1.4–1.6x. 45–75 chars per line.

days.taskTitle: Choosing Fonts for UI — Pairing Rules, Max 2 Typefaces
days.taskSub: Body: Inter, Roboto, Lato. Headings: Plus Jakarta Sans, Space Grotesk, Playfair Display. Visit fontpair.co.

days.taskTitle: Color Theory — The Color Wheel, Harmonies (Complementary, Analogous, Triadic)
days.taskSub: Tints (+ white), Tones (+ grey), Shades (+ black). Know the vocabulary — you'll use it every day.

days.taskTitle: Color in UI — 60-30-10 Rule, Semantic Colors, Color Scales
days.taskSub: Green = success. Red = error. Yellow = warning. Blue = info. Build a 50→900 scale for each color.

days.taskTitle: Contrast & Accessibility — WCAG AA Standard, Never Color Alone
days.taskSub: 4.5:1 ratio for body text. 3:1 for large text. Test at contrast.ratio.fyi right now.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 20 to 21

days.taskTitle: Exercise 3A — Build a Type Scale (20 min)
days.taskSub: Go to fonts.google.com. Choose 1 sans-serif + 1 heading font. Define H1, H2, H3, Body, Caption with size, weight, line-height. Share with Claude.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 3B — Build a Color Palette (30 min)
days.taskSub: Go to coolors.co. Generate 3 palettes for a "health & wellness" app. For each: identify primary, background, error color. Check accessibility.

days.taskTitle: Project 1-B — "Brand Identity for a Fictional App"
days.taskSub: App name + tagline + font pairing + color palette (primary, secondary, neutral, semantic) + simple logo concept. Explain every decision.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 3 + Beginner Phase Review
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 3". Then ask "Where am I in the course?" for a full progress summary.

sections.note: 🎉 Phase 1 Complete! You now think like a designer. You understand how people see, how type communicates, and how color carries meaning. Say "Start Module 4" to level up.

tabs.milestone: 🟢 Beginner Milestone: Design foundations complete — you understand UI vs UX, visual principles, typography and color. Ready for research and user thinking.

tabs.name: Phase 2 — Intermediate

sections.label: Research Foundations — Days 22 to 27

days.taskTitle: Why Research? Generative vs Evaluative Research
days.taskSub: Assumptions are the enemy of good design. Generative = before designing. Evaluative = during/after designing.

days.taskTitle: Research Methods Map — 8 Methods & When to Use Each
days.taskSub: User interviews, surveys, usability testing, card sorting, tree testing, A/B testing, contextual inquiry, diary studies.

days.taskTitle: User Interviews — The Core Skill
days.taskSub: 5 rules: open-ended questions, no leading questions, seek stories not opinions, listen 80% talk 20%, ask "why" 5 times. Practice writing good vs bad questions.

days.taskTitle: Research Synthesis — Affinity Mapping, HMW Statements, JTBD
days.taskSub: "How might we help busy parents plan healthy meals in under 5 minutes?" Turn raw observations into design opportunities.

days.taskTitle: User Personas — Components and Dangers
days.taskSub: Name, photo, demographics, goals, frustrations, behaviors, quote. Warning: assumption-based personas give false confidence.

sections.label: Psychology Principles — Days 28 to 30

days.taskTitle: Hick's Law, Fitts's Law, Miller's Law
days.taskSub: More choices = more time. Larger + closer targets are easier. Short-term memory holds ~7 items ±2. Design implications for every principle.

days.taskTitle: Jakob's Law, Peak-End Rule, Progressive Disclosure
days.taskSub: Users expect your app to work like every other app they use. They judge experiences by the peak moment and the ending.

days.taskTitle: Cognitive Load — What it is and how to always reduce it
days.taskSub: Mental effort = friction = drop-off. Every design decision either adds or removes cognitive load.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 31 to 35

days.taskTitle: Exercise 4A — Mini User Interview (1 hour)
days.taskSub: Interview 1 real person (15 min) about a daily digital habit. Prepare 5 open-ended questions. Synthesize: 3 biggest frustrations, 2 main goals.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 4B — Create a User Persona for a Recipe App
days.taskSub: Name, age, job, goal, frustration, device, quote. Label assumptions clearly if not from real research.

days.taskTitle: Project 2 — "UX Research Report" (Food/Banking/eLearning app)
days.taskSub: 8 interview questions → 2-3 real interviews → affinity mapping → 1 persona → 3 HMW statements → 2 psychology principles being violated.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 4
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 4".

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 36 to 40

days.taskTitle: Information Architecture (IA) — What it is and why it exists
days.taskSub: Users must always answer: Where am I? Where can I go? What can I do here? If they can't — your IA has failed.

days.taskTitle: Navigation Patterns — Tab Bar, Hamburger, Top Nav, Sidebar, Breadcrumbs
days.taskSub: When to use each. The hamburger menu hides features — use sparingly. Bottom tab bar: 3–5 destinations only.

days.taskTitle: Card Sorting (Open & Closed) + Tree Testing
days.taskSub: Open card sort reveals users' mental model. Tree testing validates your navigation before any UI is built.

days.taskTitle: User Flows — Task Flow vs User Flow vs Screen Flow
days.taskSub: Notation: Rectangle = screen, Diamond = decision, Arrow = action. Always include error states and alternative paths.

days.taskTitle: Sitemaps + Mental Models
days.taskSub: Build the sitemap before wireframing — it tells you what you're building. Align IA to users' mental models to reduce learning curves.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 41 to 42

days.taskTitle: Exercise 5A — Sitemap for a Task Management App (30 min)
days.taskSub: At least 4 main sections, 2–3 sub-pages each. Describe it to Claude and get structural feedback.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 5B — User Flow: New User Signs Up + Creates First Project + Adds 3 Tasks (45 min)
days.taskSub: Include happy path + at least 1 error state (e.g., passwords don't match).

days.taskTitle: Project 3 — "IA & Flows for a Fitness App"
days.taskSub: 5 core features → full sitemap → user flows for: Onboarding, Log Workout, View Progress History. Justify your navigation pattern choices.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 5
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 5".

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 43 to 49

days.taskTitle: What is Wireframing? Fidelity Levels (Lo-fi → Mid-fi → Hi-fi)
days.taskSub: Wireframes communicate structure, not beauty. Lo-fi = sketches. Mid-fi = grayscale Figma. Hi-fi = final visual design.

days.taskTitle: Paper Sketching — The Most Underrated Skill in Design
days.taskSub: Faster than any tool. Forces real thinking. Crazy 8s: fold paper into 8 panels, sketch 8 layouts in 8 minutes.

days.taskTitle: Wireframe Best Practices — Annotations, Screen States
days.taskSub: Grey boxes = images. Squiggly lines = text. Always wireframe: empty, loading, error, and success states. Real nav labels.

days.taskTitle: Lo-Fi Prototyping — Link Screens to Simulate Flow
days.taskSub: In Figma: Prototype panel → "On Click → Navigate to". Test structure and navigation before investing in visuals.

days.taskTitle: Figma Introduction — Your Primary Design Tool (Watch Official Beginner Series)
days.taskSub: Frames vs Groups, Auto Layout (most important feature!), Components & Variants, Styles, Constraints. Watch Figma's official 4-part beginner series.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 50 to 56

days.taskTitle: Exercise 6A — Crazy 8s (8 minutes!)
days.taskSub: Pick one screen from your fitness app. Set a timer. Sketch 8 completely different layouts. Pick your best and explain why.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 6B — Figma Mid-Fi Wireframe (2 hours)
days.taskSub: Create new Figma file. iPhone 14 Pro frame (393×852). Wireframe "Log Workout" screen. Only rectangles + text + icons. No color yet.

days.taskTitle: Project 4 — "Wireframe the Core 5 Screens" (Fitness App)
days.taskSub: Onboarding · Home Dashboard · Log Workout · Progress/History · Profile/Settings. Mid-fi in Figma. Annotate 3+ interactions per screen. Link in Prototype view.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 6
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 6".

phases.title: Module 7 — UI Design in Figma (Core Skills)

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 57 to 70

days.taskTitle: The Wireframe → UI Workflow (5 Steps)
days.taskSub: Start with wireframes → apply color & type → replace placeholders → refine spacing/hierarchy → add visual polish.

days.taskTitle: The 8pt Spacing System — Internal (Padding) vs External (Gap)
days.taskSub: Every space value must be a multiple of 8: 4, 8, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64. Consistency creates calm, professional designs.

days.taskTitle: UI Components & Patterns — Inputs, Navigation, Feedback, Data Display, Actions
days.taskSub: Master: text field, dropdown, checkbox, toggle, tab bar, toast, modal, card, avatar, badge, primary & secondary buttons, FAB.

days.taskTitle: States for Every Component — All 7 States
days.taskSub: Default · Hover · Active/Pressed · Focused · Disabled · Error · Loading. Every interactive element needs all states designed.

days.taskTitle: Elevation & Depth — Shadow Levels 0 to 5
days.taskSub: Shadows create visual hierarchy. Never use more than 3 elevation levels in one product. Flat (0) vs High elevation (modal = 5).

days.taskTitle: Border Radius — 0px (corporate) to full pill (tags/badges)
days.taskSub: 0px = fintech/enterprise. 4–8px = professional. 12–24px = friendly consumer apps. Full = chips, badges, toggle handles.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 69 to 77

days.taskTitle: Exercise 7A — Build Your Component Library (2–3 hours)
days.taskSub: In Figma: 1 Primary button (all 5 states) + 1 Text input (all 5 states) + 1 Card + 1 Mobile nav bar. Make them Components with Variants.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 7B — Apply Your Brand to Wireframes (3–4 hours)
days.taskSub: Take your 5 wireframes from Module 6. Apply: color palette + type scale + component library + 8pt spacing throughout.

days.taskTitle: Project 5 — "High-Fidelity Fitness App UI" (All 5 Screens)
days.taskSub: Consistent color system + typography scale + component library + proper spacing + all component states + mobile-responsive (iPhone & Android frames).

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 7
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 7".

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 78 to 86

days.taskTitle: Why Prototype? Communicating Interactions Static Screens Can't
days.taskSub: Reveals UX problems invisible in static designs. Sells your vision. Tests real behavior before any code is written.

days.taskTitle: Figma Prototyping — Triggers, Actions, Animations, Smart Animate
days.taskSub: Triggers: On Click, On Hover, On Drag, After Delay. Smart Animate: Figma's most powerful feature — animates automatically between similar frames.

days.taskTitle: Micro-interactions — 4 Parts (Dan Saffer Model)
days.taskSub: Trigger → Rules → Feedback → Loops & Modes. Examples: button press, loading spinner, toggle switch, pull-to-refresh, success transition.

days.taskTitle: Animation Principles for UI — Easing, Duration, Anticipation, Follow-Through
days.taskSub: Mobile transitions: 200–350ms. Never linear easing — always ease-in-out. Spring animation = overshoots then settles (feels alive).

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 87 to 91

days.taskTitle: Exercise 8A — Prototype Your Fitness App (2 hours)
days.taskSub: Connect all 5 screens with Smart Animate. Working nav bar on each screen. Modal overlay: "Confirm workout logged". Loading state with After Delay trigger.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 8B — Micro-interaction Study (45 min)
days.taskSub: Open Duolingo, Revolut, or Headspace. Find 5 micro-interactions. For each: What is the trigger? What is the feedback? How long does it last?

days.taskTitle: Project 6 — "Interactive Fitness App Prototype"
days.taskSub: All 5 screens connected + 3 Smart Animate transitions + 2 overlay interactions + 1 loading state. Share Figma View-Only link (or describe to Claude).

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 8
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 8".

sections.note: 🎉 Phase 2 Complete! You can now research users, architect products, wireframe, build polished UIs, and create interactive prototypes. You have a real Figma app. Say "Start Module 9" to enter Advanced territory.

tabs.milestone: 🟡 Intermediate Milestone: You have a research-backed, fully interactive Figma prototype of a fitness app. You think in flows, components, and systems.

tabs.name: Phase 3 — Advanced

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 92 to 95

days.taskTitle: Usability Testing Fundamentals — Purpose, The 5-User Rule
days.taskSub: Goal: find problems in your design, NOT prove it's good. Nielsen's rule: 5 users reveal 85% of major usability problems.

days.taskTitle: Running a Test — 5-Step Process + "Think Aloud" Protocol
days.taskSub: Define tasks → Recruit → Set the scene → Observe silently → Debrief. Never help a participant during the session.

days.taskTitle: Analyzing Results — Rainbow Spreadsheet, Severity Rating, Impact/Effort Matrix
days.taskSub: High Impact + Low Effort = do first. Rate issues 0–4. Prioritize before you fix.

days.taskTitle: Quantitative Metrics — Task Success Rate, Time on Task, SUS Score
days.taskSub: SUS = 10-question standardized survey. Score out of 100. Above 68 = "above average". Learn to calculate it.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 96 to 98

days.taskTitle: Exercise 9A — Guerrilla Usability Test (1 hour)
days.taskSub: Find 2 friends. Give them your Figma prototype link. Write 3 tasks. Observe silently — where did they hesitate? What confused them?

days.taskTitle: Exercise 9B — Calculate SUS Score
days.taskSub: Ask each participant the 10 SUS questions after the test. Tell Claude "Give me the SUS questions." Calculate and analyze your score.

days.taskTitle: Project 7 — "Usability Test Report" (3 Users)
days.taskSub: Test script + documented observations + rainbow spreadsheet + severity ratings + Impact/Effort matrix + top 3 fixes implemented + 1-page test report.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 9
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 9".

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 99 to 108

days.taskTitle: What is a Design System? (Not just a component library)
days.taskSub: Principles + tokens + components + patterns + guidelines + documentation. Study: Material Design, Apple HIG, IBM Carbon, Atlassian.

days.taskTitle: Design Tokens — Semantic Naming vs Descriptive Naming
days.taskSub: ❌ blue-500 (what it is). ✅ color-brand-primary (what it does). Color, Spacing, Typography, Elevation, Motion, Border-radius tokens.

days.taskTitle: Component Architecture — 6 Levels
days.taskSub: Token → Base Component → Composite Component → Pattern → Template → Page. Understand how they nest.

days.taskTitle: Building in Figma — Variables, Local Styles, Component Properties, Publishing
days.taskSub: Figma Variables = color, number, string, boolean. Publish your library so the whole team can use it.

days.taskTitle: Documentation + Design-to-Dev Handoff
days.taskSub: Every component needs: purpose, variants, do's & don'ts, accessibility notes. Dev Mode in Figma: code snippets, measurements, tokens.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 109 to 112

days.taskTitle: Exercise 10A — Audit a Public Design System (1 hour)
days.taskSub: Visit material.io or carbondesignsystem.com. List 5 base + 3 composite components. Study how they document a button (variants, states, usage).

days.taskTitle: Exercise 10B — Build Your Token Library in Figma (2 hours)
days.taskSub: Dedicated "Foundation" page: color variables (10-step scale) + spacing tokens (8pt) + typography styles + border radius tokens.

days.taskTitle: Project 8 — "Fitness App Design System v1.0"
days.taskSub: Foundation page (all tokens) + 10 components with 3+ states each + 3 patterns + 1-page "Getting Started" documentation + refactored screens using only library components.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 10
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 10".

phases.title: Module 11 — Advanced UI Patterns & Responsive Design

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 113 to 116

days.taskTitle: Responsive Design — Mobile-First, Breakpoints, Fluid Grids
days.taskSub: Design for the smallest screen first, scale up. Common breakpoints: 320, 375, 768, 1024, 1280, 1440px.

days.taskTitle: Responsive Layout Patterns — Column Drop, Mostly Fluid, Layout Shifter, Off Canvas
days.taskSub: Off Canvas = nav hidden off-screen, revealed by interaction. Most common on mobile. Study real examples.

days.taskTitle: Advanced UI Patterns — Infinite Scroll, Skeleton Screens, Optimistic UI, Progressive Forms
days.taskSub: Skeleton screens > spinners (reduce perceived load time). Optimistic UI assumes success before server confirms — feels instant.

days.taskTitle: Dashboard Design + Data Viz Basics + Empty States
days.taskSub: Bar (comparison), Line (trend), Pie (composition — use sparingly). Every screen needs an empty state — it's a UX opportunity, not a bug.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 117 to 119

days.taskTitle: Exercise 11A — Responsive Breakpoints for Home Dashboard (2 hours)
days.taskSub: Design at 375px (mobile), 768px (tablet), 1280px (desktop). Document how layout changes. Use Figma Auto Layout + Constraints.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 11B — 3 Empty States for Fitness App (1 hour)
days.taskSub: First-time user (no workouts yet) + no search results + network error/offline. Make them helpful and actionable.

days.taskTitle: Project 9 — "Responsive Dashboard for Fitness Coach"
days.taskSub: 5+ data visualizations + responsive mobile + desktop + 3 empty states + skeleton loading + sidebar nav (desktop) / bottom tab (mobile).

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 11
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 11".

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 120 to 124

days.taskTitle: Product Thinking — Desirability + Viability + Feasibility Triangle
days.taskSub: Understand WHY you're building before deciding WHAT to build. Great designers advocate for users while understanding business + tech constraints.

days.taskTitle: North Star Metric, OKRs, Product Roadmap Involvement
days.taskSub: Spotify's North Star: time listened per user. Your design decisions must ladder up to product objectives. You're not just executing — you're shaping.

days.taskTitle: Design Critiques — How to Give and Receive Professional Feedback
days.taskSub: Presenter: "I'm solving X for user Y, with constraint Z, because..." Reviewers: ask questions before opinions. Focus on the problem, not personal preference.

days.taskTitle: Stakeholder Management — Speaking Business Language
days.taskSub: Never say "I think it looks better." Say "Testing showed users completed the task 40% faster." Data beats opinion every time.

days.taskTitle: Metrics-Informed Design — HEART Framework + Pirate Metrics (AARRR)
days.taskSub: HEART: Happiness, Engagement, Adoption, Retention, Task Success. Know which metrics your design work moves.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 125 to 126

days.taskTitle: Exercise 12A — Product Brief for a New Fitness App Feature (1 hour)
days.taskSub: Problem statement + business goal + 3 user stories + success metrics + out of scope. Share with Claude for critique.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 12B — Design Critique Practice (live with Claude)
days.taskSub: Share any screen. Ask Claude for a formal design critique. Then respond and defend your decisions with reasoning — just like a real critique meeting.

days.taskTitle: Project 10 — "Feature Deep Dive" (New App Feature)
days.taskSub: Full product brief + user stories + success metrics + end-to-end flow + hi-fi screens + design decision document explaining every choice.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 12
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 12".

sections.label: Core Concepts — Days 127 to 130

days.taskTitle: Why Accessibility (a11y)? The Curb-Cut Effect + Legal Reality
days.taskSub: 1 in 4 adults has a disability. Accessible design benefits everyone (ramps help wheelchair users AND cyclists). WCAG 2.1 AA is legally required in many countries.

days.taskTitle: WCAG 4 Principles (POUR) — Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust
days.taskSub: These 4 principles cover every category of accessibility requirement. Understand what each demands from your designs.

days.taskTitle: Accessibility Checklist for Designers — 9 Rules
days.taskSub: 4.5:1 contrast · Never color alone · 44×44px touch targets · Visible focus states · Alt text specs · Descriptive errors · Visible labels · Logical reading order · Pausable animation.

days.taskTitle: Inclusive Design Beyond Disability — Age, Language, Context, Culture
days.taskSub: Design for someone on a slow connection, in bright sunlight, with one hand, under stress. These use cases reveal design failures that affect everyone.

sections.label: Exercises & Project — Days 131 to 133

days.taskTitle: Exercise 13A — Accessibility Audit of Your Fitness App (1 hour)
days.taskSub: Check every text for contrast ratio. Find color-only info. Find touch targets under 44×44px. Fix 3 issues. Document before/after.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 13B — Screen Reader Empathy Test (30 min)
days.taskSub: Turn on VoiceOver on your iPhone (Settings → Accessibility → VoiceOver). Navigate your most-used app for 10 minutes. Write 3 frustrations.

days.taskTitle: Project 11 — "Accessible Fitness App Redesign"
days.taskSub: Full a11y audit → fix all WCAG AA violations → add focus states → write Accessibility Annotation file for devs (alt text, aria labels, reading order) → 1-page accessibility report.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 13
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 13".

sections.note: 🎉 Phase 3 Complete! You now think at a senior designer level. You can test, build systems, handle complex UI patterns, think in product strategy, and design inclusively. One phase left: getting hired.

tabs.milestone: 🔴 Advanced Milestone: Design system built, responsive dashboard shipped, product thinking unlocked, accessibility mastered. You are now a senior-level designer in capability.

tabs.name: Phase 4 — Expert

sections.label: What Hiring Managers Care About — Days 134 to 137

days.taskTitle: What HMs Look For — Process Over Pretty Screens
days.taskSub: Problem-solving thinking. Research informing decisions. Real constraints navigated. Impact your work created. Communication skills in your case studies.

days.taskTitle: Case Study Structure — 7 Sections Every Case Study Needs
days.taskSub: 1) Overview · 2) Problem · 3) Research · 4) Process · 5) Solution · 6) Outcome · 7) Reflection. Missing any section is a red flag.

days.taskTitle: Portfolio Platforms — Webflow, Framer, Cargo, Notion, PDF
days.taskSub: 3 strong case studies beats 10 mediocre ones. 1 end-to-end project + 1 design system/B2B + 1 passion project. Custom site = memorable.

days.taskTitle: Interview Preparation — Portfolio Walkthrough, Design Challenges, Behavioral (STAR Format)
days.taskSub: Prepare a 10-min STAR-format project story. Design challenges: 30–60 min, show process out loud. Design critique: they show a real product, you analyze it.

days.taskTitle: Job Search Strategy — LinkedIn, Cold Outreach, Job Boards, Freelance Path
days.taskSub: LinkedIn: "Open to work" + keyword-rich headline. Job boards: LinkedIn, Dribbble Jobs, UX Jobs Board, AngelList. DM designers you admire for 15-min portfolio reviews.

sections.label: Build Your Portfolio — Days 138 to 140

days.taskTitle: Exercise 14A — Case Study Outline (1 hour)
days.taskSub: Write the complete outline for your Fitness App case study using all 7 sections. Share with Claude for narrative strength feedback.

days.taskTitle: Exercise 14B — Mock UX Design Interview
days.taskSub: Tell Claude: "Run a UX design interview with me." Claude asks behavioral + design questions. Get real feedback on your answers. Repeat until confident.

days.taskTitle: 🎓 FINAL PROJECT — "The Fitness App: End-to-End Case Study"
days.taskSub: Written case study (1500–2500 words) + visual deck (15–20 slides) + prototype link + outcome summary + personal reflection. This is your graduation project. Share with Claude for full professional critique before publishing.

days.taskTitle: Quiz — Module 14 + Full Course Review
days.taskSub: Tell Claude "Quiz me on Module 14". Then ask "Am I industry-ready?" for a full checklist review.

sections.note: 🏆 Course Complete. You are now an industry-ready UI/UX designer. You have a portfolio, real projects, and the thinking of a professional. Tell Claude "Recommend next steps for my career" to plan your job search.

title: AI Mastery & Prompt Engineering Roadmap
subtitle: 2 hrs/day · Phases 0–10 · Beginner → AI-Augmented Engineer · ~59 days total

tabs.name: Part 1 — Foundations (Days 1–16)

sections.label: Goal: Build the correct mental model for strategic AI use

days.taskTitle: Why This Phase Matters + The 3 Types of AI Users + The Golden Rule
days.taskSub: Copy-Paster vs Avoider vs AI-Augmented Engineer. Understand the leverage mindset deeply.

days.taskTitle: What AI Is Good At vs. Where to Be Cautious + Daily Habits + Phase 0 Exercise
days.taskSub: Write your 3-sentence answer to each exercise question. Start the Why/Verify/Improve habits today.

days.taskTitle: Common Mistakes + Resources + Phase 0 Revision Checklist
days.taskSub: Watch: Matt Wolfe (beginner-friendly AI tools). Read: Ethan Mollick's "One Useful Thing" intro post. Tick off your checklist.

sections.note: 📺 Watch: Matt Wolfe's "AI Productivity" channel intro video. Read: Ethan Mollick (One Useful Thing, Substack) — any 2 posts. Community: join r/ClaudeAI. Goal: finish Phase 0 checklist 100%.

sections.label: Goal: Understand how modern AI actually works — tokens, context, embeddings, inference

days.taskTitle: What Is an LLM? + Popular Models Overview
days.taskSub: Study the model table (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Llama, Mistral, Qwen). Understand: LLMs predict next token. Watch Karpathy "Intro to LLMs" (1 hr).

days.taskTitle: Tokens — The Fundamental Unit + Context Windows
days.taskSub: 1 token ≈ 0.75 words. Context = AI's working memory. Practice: paste text into tokenizer playground (platform.openai.com/tokenizer). Understand context degradation at the edges.

days.taskTitle: Embeddings — How AI Understands Meaning + Inference & Temperature
days.taskSub: king − man + woman ≈ queen. Try: prompt same question at temperature 0 vs 1 in Claude and compare. Understand top-p sampling.

days.taskTitle: Fine-Tuning vs. Prompting + Open-Source vs. Closed-Source AI
days.taskSub: Prompting = your primary tool. Understand cost/quality tradeoffs. Read about Llama, Mistral. Know when to use each.

days.taskTitle: Hallucinations & AI Limitations + AI Ethics Basics
days.taskSub: Test: ask Claude for 5 paper citations on a niche topic → verify each one. Experience hallucinations firsthand. Understand RLHF, alignment, bias.

days.taskTitle: Prompt Examples for LLM Understanding + Resources + Revision Checklist
days.taskSub: Try both Phase 1 prompt templates. Watch: 3Blue1Brown neural net series ep 1–2. Read Jay Alammar's "Illustrated Transformer". Tick Phase 1 checklist.

sections.note: 📺 Must watch: Andrej Karpathy "Intro to Large Language Models" (YouTube, 1 hr, beginner-friendly). Later: "Let's build GPT from scratch" (2 hr, intermediate). Read: Jay Alammar's blog (jalammar.github.io) — visual explanations. GitHub: karpathy/nanoGPT for reference.

sections.label: Goal: Master the science and art of writing prompts that produce consistently excellent results

days.taskTitle: Why Prompt Engineering Matters + The 6-Part Anatomy of a Prompt
days.taskSub: Role / Context / Task / Format / Constraints / Examples. Rewrite the "weak prompt" example yourself and compare outputs in Claude. Feel the difference.

days.taskTitle: Role Prompting + Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Prompting
days.taskSub: Try 4 different roles (Socratic tutor, harsh reviewer, rubber duck, debate opponent). Try zero-shot CoT: "Let's think step by step." Test CoT on a CS problem.

days.taskTitle: Few-Shot Prompting + System Prompts
days.taskSub: Write a few-shot prompt for a formatting task. Write your first system prompt for a "coding assistant" persona. Save it as a template.

days.taskTitle: Context Engineering Principles + Multi-Step Prompting
days.taskSub: Front-load critical info. Break a feature build into: Plan → Implement → Review → Refine. Practice the 4-step multi-step workflow from the curriculum.

days.taskTitle: Iterative Prompting + Building Super Prompts
days.taskSub: Take any Claude output → iterate 3 times with specific refinements. Study the super prompt template structure. Plan your first 3 super prompts.

days.taskTitle: Prompt Debugging + Prompt Engineering for Specific Use Cases
days.taskSub: Deliberately write a bad prompt, debug it using the 7-step process. Practice: coding prompt, research prompt, Feynman learning prompt. Try each one.

days.taskTitle: Common Mistakes + Resources + Build 3 Super Prompts + Checklist
days.taskSub: BUILD: (1) Code Reviewer super prompt. (2) Bug Debugger super prompt. (3) Concept Explainer super prompt. Save all 3 in a personal file. Tick Phase 2 checklist.

sections.note: 📺 Watch: DeepLearning.AI "ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers" (free, 1 hr). Read: Anthropic Prompt Engineering docs (docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview). GitHub: dair-ai/Prompt-Engineering-Guide. Papers: "Chain-of-Thought Prompting" (Wei et al., 2022) — abstract + summary.

tabs.milestone: Part 1 milestone: Phase 0–2 complete · 3 super prompts built · prompt debugging habit established

tabs.name: Part 2 — Productivity & Tools (Days 17–39)

sections.label: Goal: Build AI-powered systems for studying, learning, and daily productivity

days.taskTitle: CARE Framework + AI-Assisted Note-Taking System
days.taskSub: Concept → Analog → Recall → Explain back. Try: pick any CS topic → apply all 4 CARE steps using Claude. Rule: always take rough notes yourself first, then use AI for gaps.

days.taskTitle: Breaking Through Learning Blocks + Daily & Weekly AI Workflow
days.taskSub: Use the "block-busting" template: state your current understanding first. Set up your daily routine: Morning task breakdown → Pair programming → Rubber duck debug → EOD summary.

days.taskTitle: Popular AI Productivity Tools Overview + Resources + Checklist
days.taskSub: Sign up: Perplexity AI (free). Explore: NotebookLM (notebooklm.google.com). Try: ask Perplexity a research question vs Google — compare quality. Tick Phase 3 checklist.

days.taskTitle: Project: Build Your Personal AI Study System
days.taskSub: Write your own CARE super prompt. Build: daily template (morning/work/EOD). Test full workflow on a real topic you're studying now. Document it in a markdown file.

sections.note: 📺 Watch: Search "NotebookLM tutorial 2025" on YouTube. Tools to try: NotebookLM, Perplexity AI, Obsidian (optional). Read: Ethan Mollick "One Useful Thing" — any post about AI and learning. Goal: daily AI workflow running by Day 20.

sections.label: Goal: Build a professional AI-assisted coding workflow — Cursor, Claude, super prompts

days.taskTitle: Why AI Coding Tools Matter + AI Coding Toolkit Overview
days.taskSub: 30–50% faster on common tasks. Study: Cursor vs Copilot vs Codeium. Chat-based: Claude vs ChatGPT for large tasks. Specialized: v0.dev, DeepSeek Coder. Choose your stack.

days.taskTitle: Cursor IDE — Deep Dive Setup & Core Features
days.taskSub: Install Cursor. Learn: Cmd+K (inline edit), Cmd+L (chat), @file/@codebase, Composer (multi-file). Practice the full new-feature workflow from the curriculum. Read every AI-generated line.

days.taskTitle: AI Coding Super Prompt #1 — Bug Debugger + Super Prompt #2 — Code Reviewer
days.taskSub: Build both prompts fully. Test each on a real bug and a piece of your own code. Compare AI review vs your self-review. What did AI catch that you missed?

days.taskTitle: AI Coding Super Prompt #3 — Architecture Planner + Practice
days.taskSub: Build the Architecture Planner prompt. Use it to plan a small feature: identify components → edge cases → file structure → decisions. Don't write any code yet — just plan.

days.taskTitle: AI-Assisted Debugging Workflow (3 Levels) + AI for Documentation
days.taskSub: Level 1 (rubber duck) → Level 2 (code + error) → Level 3 (full context). Practice all 3 levels on a real bug. Build documentation prompt and document a function you wrote last week.

days.taskTitle: AI-Assisted Code Review Habits + Resources
days.taskSub: Rule: never merge AI code without reading every line. Ask AI: "what could go wrong with this?" Use AI to generate edge case tests. Watch a Cursor tutorial video. Read Fireship AI coding content.

days.taskTitle: Phase 4 Checklist + Finalize Your AI Coding Workflow Document
days.taskSub: Tick Phase 4 checklist. Write a 1-page "My AI Coding Workflow" doc: which tool for which task, your 3 super prompts, your debugging levels. Save it. This is your permanent reference.

days.taskTitle: Project: Build a Mini Feature End-to-End Using AI-Assisted Workflow
days.taskSub: Pick any small feature (e.g., a CLI tool, API endpoint, or simple web component). Run: Architecture Planner → Cursor Composer → Bug Debugger → Code Reviewer → Document it. Understand every line.

sections.note: 📺 Watch: "Cursor AI Tutorial 2025" (search YouTube, pick most recent). GitHub Copilot Best Practices (GitHub YouTube channel). Fireship's channel for concise AI tool overviews. Tool: install Cursor (cursor.sh) — free tier available.

sections.label: Goal: Build AI-powered workflows for reading papers, synthesizing knowledge, conducting research

days.taskTitle: The AI Research Stack + Paper Reading Workflow (Step 1 & 2)
days.taskSub: Tools: Perplexity, NotebookLM, Claude, Semantic Scholar, Elicit. Practice Step 1 (abstract scan) and Step 2 (section-by-section with AI) on any CS paper you find interesting.

days.taskTitle: Paper Reading Workflow (Step 3 — Synthesis) + NotebookLM Deep Dive
days.taskSub: Upload 3–5 related papers to NotebookLM. Ask: "main themes across these?" "where do they disagree?" Generate the audio overview. See how AI synthesizes multiple sources.

days.taskTitle: Research Super Prompts — Literature Review Starter + Research Question Refiner
days.taskSub: Build both super prompts. Use the Literature Review prompt on a topic you care about. ALWAYS verify all paper citations independently — this is non-negotiable.

days.taskTitle: Resources + Phase 5 Checklist + Full Research Workflow Exercise
days.taskSub: Exercise: choose any AI/CS topic → Perplexity for overview → NotebookLM with 4 papers → Claude for synthesis → verify 5 specific claims. Tick Phase 5 checklist.

sections.note: 📺 Watch: "NotebookLM Tutorial 2025" on YouTube. Tools: notebooklm.google.com (free), perplexity.ai (free), elicit.com, semanticscholar.org. Critical rule: ALWAYS verify AI-provided citations — hallucinated citations are common and realistic-sounding.

sections.label: Goal: Run AI locally, understand the open-source ecosystem, maintain privacy and control

days.taskTitle: Why Run AI Locally + Hardware Requirements + Quantization Explained
days.taskSub: Privacy, cost, control, offline use. Study the VRAM table. Understand quantization: Q4_K_M (recommended start) vs Q8_0 vs F16. Assess your own hardware capability.

days.taskTitle: Ollama — Install, Run 3 Different Local Models, Use the API
days.taskSub: Install Ollama. Run: llama3.2, mistral, deepseek-r1 (or phi3 if low VRAM). Test all 3 with the same prompt. Write the Python code to call Ollama API. Compare outputs.

days.taskTitle: LM Studio — GUI Workflow + Hugging Face Ecosystem Overview
days.taskSub: Download LM Studio. Browse, download, and run a model via GUI. Explore huggingface.co/models — search for "llama" and "mistral." Understand Model Hub, Datasets, Spaces, Transformers lib.

days.taskTitle: Hugging Face Transformers — Basic Python Usage + Recommended Models 2025
days.taskSub: Run the pipeline() examples (sentiment, text-gen). Study the 2025 model table. Run the same task across Llama vs Mistral vs Phi-3 — observe differences in speed and quality.

days.taskTitle: RAG Basics — How It Works + Build a Simple RAG System
days.taskSub: Understand: embed docs → vector store → retrieve → inject → generate. Implement the LangChain + FAISS + Ollama RAG code from the curriculum. Test on your own text file.

days.taskTitle: Resources + Phase 6 Revision Checklist
days.taskSub: Watch: "Run LLMs Locally with Ollama" tutorial. Read: Ollama docs + Hugging Face course intro. Tick Phase 6 checklist. Are you comfortable with local model choice for your hardware?

days.taskTitle: Project: Private Local AI Chat with RAG on Your Own Documents
days.taskSub: Extend Day 37's RAG. Feed it your own notes/lecture slides. Ask questions. Everything stays local — zero API cost, zero data leaving your machine. Push code to GitHub.

sections.note: 📺 Watch: "Ollama Tutorial" (YouTube, any 2025 version). "LM Studio Tutorial" — official or community. GitHub: ollama/ollama, huggingface/transformers, langchain-ai/langchain. Explore: huggingface.co/spaces for free hosted model demos. Course: Hugging Face NLP Course (free, huggingface.co/learn).

tabs.milestone: Part 2 milestone: Phases 3–6 complete · local AI running · RAG system built · 6+ super prompts total

tabs.name: Part 3 — Advanced & Mastery (Days 40–59)

sections.label: Goal: Combine multiple AI models strategically — choose the right tool for every task

days.taskTitle: The Multi-Model Mindset + Model Selection Guide
days.taskSub: Study the full task → model table. No single model is best at everything. Build your personal model selection guide (a markdown table). Which model for: long docs, coding, math, vision, privacy?

days.taskTitle: OpenRouter — One API for 100+ Models
days.taskSub: Sign up at openrouter.ai. Run the Python code example from the curriculum. Switch between Claude, GPT-4o, and Llama with one line change. Compare cost per model. Understand fallback routing.

days.taskTitle: AI Tool Chaining — Blog Workflow + Code Review Workflow
days.taskSub: Map out both multi-tool chains. Practice: Perplexity → Claude for a technical blog post. Practice: Cursor → Claude architecture review → DeepSeek optimization check. Feel the power of chaining.

days.taskTitle: Comparing Models for the Same Task + Resources + Checklist
days.taskSub: Run the same complex prompt through Claude, GPT-4o (or ChatGPT), and a local model. Document: reasoning style, accuracy, format, edge cases. Visit lmarena.ai for side-by-side comparisons. Tick Phase 7 checklist.

days.taskTitle: Project: Build a 2+ Tool Chain for a Real Personal Task
days.taskSub: Examples: Research assistant (Perplexity → Claude → NotebookLM). Or: Code pipeline (Cursor → Claude review → local model for privacy-sensitive logic). Document the chain and push to GitHub.

sections.note: 📺 Watch: Latent Space podcast (any recent episode on multi-model workflows). Tools: openrouter.ai, lmarena.ai. Community: r/LocalLLaMA — active open-source model community. Goal: your personal model selection guide should be written and tested by Day 44.

sections.label: Goal: Understand and build basic AI agents, automation workflows, and RAG systems

days.taskTitle: What Are AI Agents? — Agent vs. Chatbot Mental Model
days.taskSub: Chatbot: ask → answer → done. Agent: give goal → plan → multi-step execution → tools → report. Study: agents browse web, write/execute code, call APIs. Watch: "AI Agents Explained" (YouTube).

days.taskTitle: LangChain Basics — Chains: Prompt → LLM → Output
days.taskSub: Install LangChain: pip install langchain langchain-anthropic. Run the simple chain example from the curriculum. Understand: prompt template → llm → chain.invoke(). Modify: change the system prompt, change the model.

days.taskTitle: LangChain Agents — Tools, ReAct, AgentExecutor
days.taskSub: Run the DuckDuckGo search agent example. Understand ReAct: Reason → Act → Observe → repeat. Add a second tool (calculator or code execution). Watch the verbose=True output — see the agent's reasoning.

days.taskTitle: RAG — Advanced Production Architecture + Vector Databases
days.taskSub: Study the full 5-step production RAG pipeline. Understand vector DB tradeoffs: FAISS (local) vs Chroma (persistent) vs Pinecone (cloud). Build a persistent Chroma RAG that survives restarts.

days.taskTitle: AI Automation Workflows — No-Code/Low-Code Tools
days.taskSub: Explore: n8n (open-source, self-hostable) or Make (Integromat). Build one simple automation: trigger (webhook/email) → AI step (summarize/classify) → output (file/message). Understand visual AI pipelines.

days.taskTitle: Resources + Phase 8 Revision Checklist
days.taskSub: Watch: LangChain official YouTube channel (pick most relevant tutorial). Read: LangChain docs. Explore: Flowise (visual LangChain builder). Tick Phase 8 checklist. Reflect on what you built.

days.taskTitle: Project: Build a LangChain Agent with Web Search + RAG
days.taskSub: Combine: web search tool + your Chroma RAG knowledge base + Claude. Ask the agent a complex question it must research + answer using both tools. Push to GitHub with a clear README.

sections.note: 📺 Watch: DeepLearning.AI "LangChain for LLM Application Development" (free). GitHub: langchain-ai/langchain, langflow-ai/langflow (visual builder), chroma-core/chroma. Tools: n8n.io (open-source automation), flowise.ai (visual LangChain). Papers: "Retrieval Augmented Generation" (Lewis et al., 2020) — read abstract.

sections.label: Goal: Position yourself strategically as an AI-augmented engineer in the job market

days.taskTitle: What Will AI Change in Software Engineering? Roles & Skills
days.taskSub: Study: which roles grow (ML Eng, architect, domain expert + AI). Which skills grow (system design, decomposition, verification, communication). Which habits make engineers obsolete. Be brutally honest about where you are.

days.taskTitle: Building an AI-Augmented Portfolio — Project Ideas + Planning
days.taskSub: Choose 1–2 portfolio projects from the list (RAG app, multi-agent system, AI productivity tool, fine-tuned model). Write a plan: what you'll build, what it demonstrates, what AI workflow you'll use.

days.taskTitle: Interview Prep with AI + Staying Current in the AI Landscape
days.taskSub: Run the AI mock interview prompt. Set up: AI Twitter/X follows (Karpathy, Mollick, Simonw). Subscribe: The Batch (DeepLearning.AI), ImportAI newsletter. Bookmark: huggingface.co/papers for daily papers.

days.taskTitle: Resources + Phase 9 Checklist + Write Your 90-Day Action Plan
days.taskSub: Write a concrete 90-day plan: what you'll build, what you'll learn, which communities you'll join, which newsletters you'll read weekly. Tick Phase 9 checklist. Share your plan somewhere to create accountability.

sections.note: 📺 Communities: Latent Space Discord, r/MachineLearning. Newsletters: The Batch (deeplearning.ai/the-batch), ImportAI (Jack Clark), TLDR AI. Daily: huggingface.co/papers — skim titles and read 1 abstract per day. Goal: your staying-current system must be running by Day 55.

sections.label: Goal: Integrate everything into a cohesive identity, stack, and permanent engineering practice

days.taskTitle: The AI-Augmented Engineer Manifesto + The Compound Effect
days.taskSub: Read the 7-point manifesto. Honestly grade yourself on each point (1–10). The compound effect: 15 min/day on prompt library × 6 months = massive advantage. Write what you'll do daily/weekly going forward.

days.taskTitle: Build Your Personal AI Stack Document
days.taskSub: Create the "My AI Stack" markdown document from the template. Fill every section: daily tools, task-specific tools, super prompts library, workflows, models evaluated, what you're currently exploring. This is your living reference.

days.taskTitle: The Future AI Engineer: A Realistic Picture + Final Revision Checklist
days.taskSub: Read the 2025→2030+ picture. Final checklist: all 12 items. Be honest — which are not yet 100%? Mark those as ongoing goals, not failures. The checklist is never "done" — it evolves.

days.taskTitle: Capstone: AI-Augmented Project + Reflection + GitHub Push
days.taskSub: Complete or significantly advance your Phase 9 portfolio project. Write a 500-word reflection: what changed in how you think about AI? What are your 3 strongest new habits? Where will you be in 6 months? Push everything to GitHub.

sections.note: 🎯 Final Goal: You now have — a personal AI stack, 10+ super prompts, a daily/weekly workflow, a portfolio project, a staying-current system, and the mindset of an AI-augmented engineer. This curriculum ends but the practice never does. Revisit Phase 10 every 3 months and update your stack.

tabs.milestone: COMPLETE — All 10 Phases done · AI-Augmented Engineer identity established · ~59 days · 118 hours
